observations.recommendation: Instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly monitor the acceptability of supporting documents used in the liquidation of government funds to properly establish the occurrence of transactions and ultimately, the extinguishment of liability.
observations.managementAction: BIR 2307 Forms and Proof of receipt of payments were not attached.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
The waterwork service office:
Submit a monthly report on collection and outstanding billings vital for monitoring delinquent concessionaire receivables;
Send demand letters on concessionaires with long-outstanding liabilities providing there for a specific period within which to settle the payables;
In case of failure to settle, the issuance of disconnection notice shall ensue;
Management establishes a written policy manual relative to connection, disconnection, and reconnection to water supply to be reviewed and adopted by the local sanggunian;

The Sangguniang Bayan:
Review the existing local ordinances on the water system management and update accordingly to conform with the present needs and circumstances;
Conduct an extensive review on the policy manual to be endorsed by the executive department for adoption as warranted;

Require the Local Disaster Reduction Management Council (LDRRMC) to review the existing PPAs under the LDRRMF to determine the relevance and responsiveness of the projects to the present circumstances including the reasonableness of project costing and make the necessary revisions as warranted subject to the appropriate approvals;

Ensure that PPAs funded out of the DRRM funds are well-planned and meticulously reviewed by the LDRRMC. Accordingly, intensive procurement planning and coordination with stakeholders shall be conducted prior to the inclusion of PPAs in the LDRRMP and LDRRMFIP;
observations.reason: Management had an overall low utilization. Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: Ensure the utilization of the continuing funds maintained under special trust fund and, if necessary, the LDRRMC perform reprogramming of the unexpended fund into a more attainable PPAs that support disaster risk management activities to further strengthen the municipalities resilience towards disaster; and
observations.reason: The agency registered a zero utilization on the continuing appropriations under the LDRRMF. The recommendation was reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: Strictly monitor the implementation of the PPAs to facilitate the timely completion thereof but indicating measurable performance indicators.
observations.reason: Reiterated in part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the OIC-Municipal Engineer in coordination with the Local Finance Committee to evaluate the feasibility of implementing the identified projects with fund transfers totaling ₱5.750 million which remained unimplemented as at year-end. If to the negative, Management shall request the source agencies for the authority to reprogram the funds for other purposes;
observations.reason: The fund remained unutilized as at year-end. Included in the audit of the “Due to NGAs” account as elaborated and reiterated in part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: require the Municipal Engineer to submit to the Accounting Office and Office of the Auditor the duly supported status reports on the “various LGU Projects” funded by the DOH with unexpended balance of ₱0.264 million as at year-end;
observations.reason: The amount is still included in the Fs and schedules. Included in the audit of the “Due to NGAs” account as elaborated and reiterated in part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: require the Acting Municipal Accountant to return immediately to the Source Agencies or to the National Treasury, as applicable, the unexpended balances of the fund transfers with specific provisions on the MOA or its equivalent as regards the disposition thereof. Similar actions shall also be made on the balances without available covering agreements;
observations.reason: While actions have been taken to return to the National Treasury unexpended balances, several amounts due for reversion, were still noted during the audit of the:” Due to NGAs” account. Reiterated in part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: require the MLGU-BEA, through its President, to immediately secure the necessary registration certificates from the Securities and Exchange Commission and Bureau of Internal Revenue. Accordingly, a BIR-approved Official Receipt shall be issued for each collection to be made by the Association;
observations.reason: While SEC Registration has been secured, the BIR-approved official receipt is yet to be obtained.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Direct the Accounting Office to conduct further review and assessment of the dormant “Loans Receivable-Others” account and record the additional allowance for impairment with the following entries:
Demand from the concerned official the immediate and full liquidation of the outstanding cash advances aged below ten years;
Institute the withholding of the salaries of accountable officers who failed to liquidate his/her cash advances within the prescribed period pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly review and monitor the status of the “Loans Receivable-Others” account and observe the guidelines and procedures in the proper disposition of dormant accounts as prescribed in COA issuances.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments to correct the reported balances of affected accounts in the FS. Accordingly, working paper adjustment entries shall be prepared for the restatement of the affected accounts to present the correct CY 2024 restated figures in the preparation of the CY 2025 comparative FS. Henceforth, all delivered construction materials should be initially recorded under the Construction Materials Inventory account upon delivery and should only be derecognized when the materials are actually issued to a specific project;
Direct the General Services Office (GSO), in coordination with the Inventory Committee, to maintain and update stock cards for the discovered construction materials; and
Instruct the Inventory Committee to subject all existing inventory to semi-annual physical count and coordinate with representatives from the Accounting Office and Commission on Audit (COA) for witnessing the physical count.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
Conduct an immediate reconciliation between the book records, the Summary of LDRRMF Prepositioned Goods, and the RPCI. Any discrepancies identified, such as unrecorded or erroneously recorded items, should be supported with appropriate documentation (e.g., issuance slips, inventory transfer reports) and properly adjusted through journal entries;
Reclassify the relief goods intended for distribution to disaster-affected individuals under "Welfare Goods for Distribution" (1-04-02-020) in compliance with the Revised Chart of Accounts; and
Effect the necessary adjustments to correct the reported balances of affected accounts in the FS. Accordingly, working paper adjustment entries shall be prepared for the restatement of the affected accounts to present the correct CY 2024 restated figures in the preparation of the CY 2025 comparative FS.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments to correct the reported balances of affected accounts in the FS. Accordingly, working paper adjustment entries shall be prepared for the restatement of the affected accounts to present the correct CY 2024 restated figures in the preparation of the CY 2025 comparative FS; and
Instruct the GSO, in coordination with the Inventory Committee, to prepare IIRUP and furnish a copy to the Accounting Office. Henceforth, the Accounting Office shall conduct impairment testing for unserviceable property not yet disposed as of year-end based on submitted IIRUP and recognize impairment losses, if any.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to update the balances of biological assets in the ledgers, take up the proposed adjusting entries, and submit the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to the Office of the Auditor; and
Direct the Agriculture Office to conduct the reappraisal before year-end and submit the report thereon to the Municipal Accountant to provide ample time in updating the balances in the agency’s books.

We recommended that Management revert the outstanding Accounts Payable amounting to ₱22,927.15 to the unappropriated surplus. Accordingly, a journal entry should be prepared by debiting Accounts Payable and crediting Government Equity for the said amount.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
Reclassify the amount of ₱0.874 million representing the 10% Retention Money from the ‘Other Payables’ account to the ‘Guaranty/Security Deposit Payable’ account to ensure that liabilities are properly presented in accordance with their nature; and
Strictly observe the proper classification of accounts in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs and the provisions of Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1, to ensure the fair presentation of FS and enhance the reliability and accuracy of reported financial information.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the MDRRMC and the LDC to ensure that all PPAs are meticulously and judiciously planned by providing rigid review and evaluation; and
Require the MDRRMC to conduct regular review and monitoring of the implementation of PPAs and update the PPMP as necessary to ensure that funds are fully utilized. Relative to this, strictly ensure the effective and efficient implementation of PPAs to ensure that the fund’s purpose of strengthening the agency’s capabilities in mitigating, responding, and recovering from the impact of disasters are satisfactorily attained.
We recommended that Management continuously monitor the implementation of GAD-related PPAs to sustain a high utilization rate, thereby maximizing the benefits for the target beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to prioritize the immediate reconciliation of the “Due to Pag-IBIG” account to determine whether the ₱56,717.31 balance represents a valid obligation and to establish the accuracy of the account. Once confirmed, appropriate adjustments or payments should be made without further delay. Additionally, a periodic reconciliation process should be instituted to ensure that remittances are accurately and promptly recorded, preventing the accumulation of unresolved balances in future periods.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant and Chairperson, Search for Miss Batanes Committee to require the payees to submit proof evidencing receipt of the payments totaling ₱0.141 million bearing the information required in the COA Circular No. 2004-006 dated September 9, 2004. Otherwise, submit an explanation/justification for the absence thereof;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Office of the Auditor the duly accomplished BIR Form No. 2307 applicable to the subject transactions; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the BIR Form No. 2307 is duly acknowledged and received by the income payee for every transaction where expanded withholding tax (EWT) system is applied.

Direct the “Search for Miss Batanes” Committee to provide explanation/justification on the unsupported payments and the temporary receipts not bearing the minimum data required by COA Circular No. 2004-006 dated September 9, 2004, totaling ₱41,161.00. Also, provide sufficient and verifiable evidence that the amounts were actually received by the payees and the related liabilities were extinguished;
observations.managementAction: 2024 transactions for Miss Batanes were made thru applicable modalities

observations.recommendation: Require the concerned office to incorporate in its regular budget proposal a project/program/activity which shall include detailed funding and budgeting for the Miss Batanes competition since the activity is annually conducted province-wide. Relative thereto, proper procurement planning and applicable procurement modalities shall be employed in the procurement of goods and services necessary in implementing the activity; and
observations.managementAction: 2024 transactions for Miss Batanes were made thru applicable modalities

observations.recommendation: Craft an ordinance pertaining to the distribution and control over water meters procured by the agency as this are vital equipment relevant to the management of water supply service in the municipality;

Intensify the procurement planning by conducting trend analysis to determine activity requirements specifically on the meals and snacks and consider entering into a Framework Agreement, as allowed in Appendix 32, 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 following the procedures for competitive bidding;
observations.managementAction: Starting the 2nd semester of 2024, the management already implemented the recommendations of the Commission.

observations.recommendation: Adopt the implementation of EPA for the public bidding on the procurement of meals and snacks under a Framework Agreement to cover annually recuring activities such as business one-stop shop, quarterly meeting of GAD, and other similar activities determinable by Management; and
observations.managementAction: Starting the 2nd semester of 2024, the management already implemented the recommendations of the Commission.

observations.recommendation: Refrain from the practice of reimbursement of representation expenses except for exceptional cases such as: (a) Section 53.2 Negotiated Procurement thru Emergency Cases and (b) Per diems in connection to official travels.
observations.managementAction: Starting the 2nd semester of 2024, the management already implemented the recommendations of the Commission.

observations.recommendation: Craft a policy prescribing the guidelines on the grant of honoraria to the BAC members and the TWG, specifically stating that complete attendance on the duration of the procurement process shall be basis of the payment of honoraria to facilitate just and equal compensation to all the BAC members
observations.managementAction: Internal policy were already created for the grant of BAC honoraria.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Engineering Office to conduct, on a timely manner, the detailed engineering activities and Preparation of POWs of Infrastructure projects to preclude the recurrence of non-inclusion of the general components in the indicative PPMP during the Budget Preparation Phase, thereby increasing the CY 20% DF utilization rate at year-end;
observations.managementAction: PPAs that were reprogrammed were already implemented in 2024

observations.recommendation: Advise the reviewing personnel/bodies including the members of the Sangguniang Bayan to take active participation in rigorously evaluating the propriety, accuracy and completeness of each Department’s budget proposals, to ensure that only relevant, responsive and feasible items are to be funded and implemented out of the 20% DF. Accordingly, all infrastructure projects shall be supported with POWs and DEDs during the presentation of Budget Proposals;
observations.managementAction: PPAs that were reprogrammed were already implemented in 2024

observations.recommendation: Ensure that all PPAs are meticulously and judiciously planned by rigidly screening PPAs to be funded out of the 20% DF especially during the preparation of budget proposal. Accordingly, require the implementing offices to propose projects that are well-planned and procurement-and implementation-ready;
observations.managementAction: PPAs that were reprogrammed were already implemented in 2024

observations.recommendation: Require the BAC and the MPDO to properly plan all its procurement activities, particularly the procurement of infrastructure projects taking into consideration the period of implementation to avoid delays and suspensions;
observations.managementAction: PPAs that were reprogrammed were already implemented in 2024

observations.recommendation: Advise the MPDO together with the concerned implementing offices, in coordination with the Bids and Awards Committee, to conduct further review of the unimplemented projects under the 20% Development Fund. Formulate/develop specific strategies/solutions to effectively implement development projects;
observations.managementAction: PPAs that were reprogrammed were already implemented in 2024

observations.recommendation: Direct the implementing offices, to communicate with the regulating agencies to expedite the acquisition of the required clearances. Accordingly, Management shall endeavor to complete all the appropriate preparatory activities such as, but not limited to, cost-benefit analysis, feasibility studies, market research, and detailed engineering designs, regulatory clearances to preclude delays in the implementation of the PPAs;
observations.managementAction: PPAs that were reprogrammed were already implemented in 2024

observations.recommendation: Henceforth, intensify the close monitoring of the implementation of PPAs under the 20% DF to attain the desired benefits derived from the implementation of these programs, projects and activities.
observations.managementAction: PPAs that were reprogrammed were already implemented in 2024

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the implementing offices to propose projects that are well-planned and procurement-and implementation-ready. Henceforth, rigid screening of PPAs to be funded out of the 20% DF shall be employed to ensure that only relevant, responsive and feasible items are to be funded and implemented;

require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator together with the concerned implementing offices, in coordination with the Local Finance Committee, to conduct a periodic review and assessment of the financial and physical status of the programs, projects and activities (PPAs) to determine timely the possible declaration of savings which will be reappropriated for other development projects under the 20% DF, and formulate catch-up plans for slow-moving PPAs to avoid further delays in the implementation;

require the implementing offices, in coordination with Accounting Office, to fast track the preparation of necessary documents for claims that are due and demandable, thereby increasing the utilization rate of the PPAs as of year-end.

request authority from the Source Agencies to transfer the unexpended balances from the Trust Fund to the General Fund for the fund MOA or its equivalent containing no provision on the disposition of unexpended amounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, otherwise cause the remittance thereof to the source or the National Treasury;

require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the implementing offices to propose projects that are well-planned and procurement-and implementation-ready. Henceforth, rigid screening of PPAs to be funded out of the 20% DF shall be employed to ensure that only relevant, responsive and feasible items are to be funded and implemented;

observations.recommendation: require the MLGU-BEA, through its President, to immediately secure the necessary registration certificates from the Securities and Exchange Commission and Bureau of Internal Revenue. Accordingly, a BIR-approved Official Receipt shall be issued for each collection to be made by the Association;
observations.managementAction: Application for BIR certificate to process.
observations.reason: Recent receipt of the required SEC Certificate needed for the BIR certificate application.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management continue performing its good practices, strictly implement and observe appropriate controls and pursue to improve the processes and areas that were noted to be non-compliant to sound internal controls in the previously issued Audit Observation Memoranda.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the “Search for Miss Batanes” Committee to provide explanation/justification on the unsupported payments and the temporary receipts not bearing the minimum data required by COA Circular No. 2004-006 dated September 9, 2004, totaling ₱41,161.00. 
Also, provide sufficient and verifiable evidence that the amounts were actually received by the payees and the related liabilities were extinguished;
Require the concerned office to incorporate in its regular budget proposal a project/program/activity which shall include detailed funding and budgeting for the Miss Batanes competition since the activity is annually conducted province-wide. Relative thereto, proper procurement planning and applicable procurement modalities shall be employed in the procurement of goods and services necessary in implementing the activity; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly monitor the acceptability of supporting documents used in the liquidation of government funds to properly establish the occurrence of transactions and ultimately, the extinguishment of liability.

We recommended that Management:
Intensify the procurement planning by conducting trend analysis to determine activity requirements specifically on the meals and snacks and consider entering into a Framework Agreement, as allowed in Appendix 32, 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 following the procedures for competitive bidding;
Adopt the implementation of EPA for the public bidding on the procurement of meals and snacks under a Framework Agreement to cover annually recurring activities such as business one-stop shop, quarterly meeting of GAD, and other similar activities determinable by Management; and
Refrain from the practice of reimbursement of representation expenses except for exceptional cases such as: (a) Section 53.2 Negotiated Procurement thru Emergency Cases and (b) Per diems in connection to official travels.

We recommended that Management craft a policy prescribing the guidelines on the grant of honoraria to the BAC members and the TWG, specifically stating that complete attendance on the duration of the procurement process shall be basis of the payment of honoraria to facilitate just and equal compensation to all the BAC members.

direct the Municipal Engineer, during the budget preparation for the ensuing year, to prepare the Program of Works (POWs) for infrastructure projects prior to inclusion of the PPAs in the Annual Investment Program (AIP);

require the implementing offices, in coordination with Bids and Awards Committee, to observe the timely procurement activities to ensure prompt delivery of materials and other items necessary in the implementation of PPAs, thus reducing the risk of delayed implementation. Accordingly, Management shall endeavor to conduct early procurement activities pursuant to the provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of Republic Act No. 9184;
require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to remit to the National Treasury the ₱27,041.20 unutilized cash donations; and

direct the parties involved in the preparation of the LDRRM budget to observe the documentary requirements and procedures prescribed in COA Circular No.  2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 for the implementation of programs, projects and activities.
ensure that material information are supplied on the Agreements/Contracts entered into by the Agency.

in coordination with the local sanggunian, revisit and determine the propriety of amending the Memorandum of Agreement and the related Resolution on the management of the Batanes Marine Sanctuary specifically on the sharing ratio and basis of the LGU-share and install appropriate safeguards thereof and submit to the Office of the Auditor the revised versions, if warranted;

pending the issuance of the requisite registration certificates, request from the local sanggunian an authority to collect fees on behalf of the organization which shall be turned over to the latter upon compliance of its registration requirements with the regulatory agencies; and
direct the President of MLGU-BEA to remit immediately to the Municipal Treasury Office the ₱79,244.50 representing 50% LGU-Share as of December 31, 2022.

We recommended Management to submit their basis in the grant of calamity/pandemic relief assistance to officers and employees of the Municipality. Likewise, succeeding donations should not be made for purposes other than what is allowed by existing government rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted an Ordinance as legal basis.
observations.reason: The Ordinance is not sufficient legal basis, hence the Calamity/Pandemic Relief Assistance is disallowable. Reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to observe year-end cutoff procedures by furnishing the Accounting Office with the Schedule of Unreleased Checks. Accordingly, the Acting Municipal Accountant shall prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to revert the amount representing unreleased checks to the Cash in Bank and Payable accounts;
require the Acting Municipal Accountant to analyze the nature of the transactions covered by the 36 stale checks with due consideration on the availability of documents to support the same. The Acting Municipal Accountant shall inform and require the concerned payees to submit the checks issued for possible replacement. For transactions with complete documents, adjusting entry shall be prepared to revert the same to the Payable accounts. On the other hand, the transactions with incomplete supporting documents shall be closed to Government Equity; and
advise the Acting Municipal Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements for the period October to December 2021 and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor for verification. Henceforth, the prompt preparation and submission of BRS shall be observed.

We recommended that Management:
prioritize the strict implementation and enforcement of the documentary and procedural requirements of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances;
require the Inventory Committee and Acting Municipal Accountant to identify  the PPE items that failed to meet the P15,000.00 capitalization threshold and the former shall prepare a revised RPCPPE and the latter shall prepare appropriate adjusting entries based on verifiable evidence; and
direct the Inventory Committee to submit the list of property that were damaged by the Super Typhoon “Kiko” to the Accounting Office. The Appraisal Team shall determine the value of the said assets and the Acting Municipal Accountant shall recognize impairment loss, as appropriate. A copy of the journal entry voucher and its supporting documents shall be furnished to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that Management:
require the Agency’s Inventory Committee, Appraisal Committee in coordination with the Municipal Agriculturist to  ascertain the fair value less cost to sell of the identified biological assets and prepare a report thereon to be submitted to the Accounting Office;
direct the Municipal Accountant, based on the report to be prepared by the concerned personnel, to prepare adjusting entries to reflect the correct balance of the “Breeding Stock” account. Henceforth, biological assets, at each reporting date, shall be measured at fair value less cost to sell as required in IPSAS 27; and
henceforth, advise the Municipal Agriculturist to regularly furnish the report on the changes in the fair value of the biological assets of the Agency to ensure up-to-date fair value measurement of the biological assets.

We recommended that Management:
provide an explanation/justification with verifiable basis why the payment of Calamity Relief Assistance for CYs 2020 and 2021 which was noted as non-compliant with existing laws and COA rules and regulations, as mentioned above, should not be disallowed in post-audit. Otherwise, require the immediate refund of the amount received by all payees; and
discontinue granting benefits to its employees without sufficient and valid legal basis.

We recommended that Management require the identified officials and employees to file an application for bonding with the Bureau of the Treasury-Batanes Provincial Office, strictly monitor the expiration of bond coverage and apply for renewal thereof prior to its expiration as required in Treasury Circular No. 02-2019 dated April 25, 2019 with due consideration on the processing time from the application to the approval of the bond.

We recommended that Management direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to transmit to the Office of the Auditor, within five days after the execution of the contract, all documents to support the perfection thereof as  enumerated in COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. A list of the documents submitted shall accompany the transmittal for ease of verification. Accordingly, a copy of the COA-received transmittal shall form part of the integral supporting documents in the processing of the related disbursement vouchers.

We recommended that Management:
direct all individual service providers under Job Order who are deriving income below P250,00.00 to execute an Income Payee’s Sworn Declaration of gross receipts/sales (Annex B2 of RR 11-2018 or Annex A1 of RMC 51-2018) and pay the Annual Registration Fee the proofs of which shall be submitted to the Municipal Human Resource Management Office;
require the authorized representative of the Agency, after submission by the service providers of the duly accomplished declaration, to execute the Income Payor/Withholding Agent’s Sworn Declaration (Annex C of RR 11-2018) and submit together with the list of payees who shall not be subjected to withholding taxes to the Bureau of Internal Revenue; and
require the Municipal Human Resource Office to strictly observe the annual compliance deadlines as prescribed by RR 11-2018. Accordingly, the requirements shall be submitted by the individual service providers before January 15 or prior to initial income payment, whichever is applicable. The documentary requirements of the Agency as withholding agent shall be submitted to the BIR  office on or before the last day of January of each year or on the fifteenth (15th) day of the following month when a new income recipient submitted the payee’s sworn declaration to the lone income payor/withholding agent.

We recommended that the Engineering Office closely monitor the projects and maintain records for reconciliation with the subsidiary ledgers of the Accounting Office. Likewise, trace and verify the status and history of these accounts, transfer completed projects to Public Infrastructure account and their respective registries and to make the necessary adjustments relative to the current and prior years unrecorded depreciation expense of any finished CIP-agency assets.
observations.managementAction: A re-inspection and validation of completed projects are being conducted by the Engineering Office and the Inspectorate Team as a prerequisite to the issuance of Certificates of Completion to ensure accurate recording and reconciliation of records and update unadjusted balances under the Construction in Progress account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Agriculturist to validate and investigate the cause of death of said animals owned by the Municipality and if found meritorious, file for a request for relief from accountability duly supported with the required documents under Sections 151 and 152 of COA Circular No. 92-386. Otherwise, the amount of the disposed animals shall be refunded by the concerned municipal officials and/or program beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Agriculture Office already submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to carefully identify the priority needs of the Municipality and timely implement these projects to attain their objectives. Furthermore, conduct intensive review of the continuing projects funded under the 20% DF to determine if the PPAs are still part of the vital needs of the Municipality otherwise, realign or reprogram the identified PPA to priority PPAs in accordance with DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017. The establishment of sanitary landfill should be given priority.
observations.managementAction:
The establishment of sanitary landfill is pending due to the consultancy services for its technical design.

Some of the 20% Development Fund projects were already completed. The Balance Represents excess or savings for each program. Appropriation Ordinance No. 2021-01 for the supplemental budget of consultancy services was passed to fund the program.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other local officials concerned timely implement projects funded from LGSF in the year it was granted to achieve the optimum benefits and purpose of which the fund was created. Likewise, adequately plan so as to anticipate identifiable problems that will potentially cause the delay of project implementation.

We also recommended that the LGU make representation with the source agency to make amendments on the identified project. Request to use the fund for more sustainable project.
observations.reason: Identified project is not viable

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Engineering Office closely monitor the projects and maintain records for reconciliation with the subsidiary ledgers of the Accounting Office. Likewise, trace and verify the status and history of these accounts, transfer completed projects to Public Infrastructure account and their respective registries and to make the necessary adjustments relative to the current and prior years unrecorded depreciation expense of any finished CIP-agency assets.

We recommended Management to submit their basis in the grant of calamity/pandemic relief assistance to officers and employees of the Municipality. Likewise, succeeding donations should not be made for purposes other than what is allowed by existing government rules and regulations.

We recommended the Municipal Agriculturist to validate and investigate the cause of death of said animals owned by the Municipality and if found meritorious, file for a request for relief from accountability duly supported with the required documents under Sections 151 and 152 of COA Circular No. 92-386. Otherwise, the amount of the disposed animals shall be refunded by the concerned municipal officials and/or program beneficiaries.

We recommended Management to carefully identify the priority needs of the Municipality and timely implement these projects to attain their objectives. Furthermore, conduct intensive review of the continuing projects funded under the 20% DF to determine if the PPAs are still part of the vital needs of the Municipality otherwise, realign or reprogram the identified PPA to priority PPAs in accordance with DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017. The establishment of sanitary landfill should be given priority.

We recommended that the LCE, together with members of the Municipal Waste Management Board, appropriate enough funds for its Solid Waste Management program specially in the establishment of a sanitary landfill to ensure the protection of the public health and environment of the locality.
observations.reason: Procurement of consultant already on-going

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive expedite the implementation of all priority development projects and activities for a timely realization of the expected benefits that could be derived thereat by the constituents. Likewise, the office/s concerned in the preparation and identification of the municipality’s priority projects must consider proper planning to come up with more realistic and timely PPAs.
observations.reason: Procurement already on-going

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to transfer to the agencies concerned the book value of all properties being used and operated by agencies other than the Local Government Unit of Basco to conform with IPSAS 17 – Property, Plant and Equipment. The Accounting Office should draw the necessary Journal Entry Voucher to derecognize the PPE transferred to other agencies and furnish a copy of the JEV, together with the Inventory Listing, to the recipient agencies in order for those agencies to record the same in their books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Management already transferred the equipment to the schools.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE establish a GAD Monitoring and Evaluation System as required under Items 3.4 and 5.1 of PCW-DILD-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013.01. The Team shall regularly convene in order that it may be able to effectively perform its functions in accordance with the JMC.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to analyze the affected accounts to determine to which funds the amounts are due and prepare necessary adjusting journal entries to reflect the correct balances of each accounts in their respective fund classification and conduct periodic reconciliation/analysis, preferably on a quarterly basis, of the reciprocal accounts “Due from/Due to Other Funds” to ensure that necessary adjustments in the books of accounts are made on time.

1.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other local officials concerned timely implement projects funded from LGSF in the year it was granted to achieve the optimum benefits and purpose of which the fund was created. Likewise, adequately plan so as to anticipate identifiable problems that will potentially cause the delay of project implementation.
1.9 We also recommended that the LGU make representation with the source agency to make amendments on the identified project. Request to use the fund for more sustainable project.

2.13 We recommended that the LCE, together with members of the Municipal Waste Management Board, appropriate enough funds for its Solid Waste Management program specially in the establishment of a sanitary landfill to ensure the protection of the public health and environment of the locality.

3.5 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive expedite the implementation of all priority development projects and activities for a timely realization of the expected benefits that could be derived thereat by the constituents. Likewise, the office/s concerned in the preparation and identification of the municipality’s priority projects must consider proper planning to come up with more realistic and timely PPAs.

4.4 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to transfer to the agencies concerned the book value of all properties being used and operated by agencies other than the Local Government Unit of Basco to conform with IPSAS 17 – Property, Plant and Equipment. The Accounting Office should draw the necessary Journal Entry Voucher to derecognize the PPE transferred to other agencies and furnish a copy of the JEV, together with the Inventory Listing, to the recipient agencies in order for those agencies to record the same in their books of accounts.

5.5 We recommended that the LCE establish a GAD Monitoring and Evaluation System as required under Items 3.4 and 5.1 of PCW-DILD-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013.01. The Team shall regularly convene in order that it may be able to effectively perform its functions in accordance with the JMC.

6.6 We recommended the Municipal Accountant to analyze the affected accounts to determine to which funds the amounts are due and prepare necessary adjusting journal entries to reflect the correct balances of each accounts in their respective fund classification and conduct periodic reconciliation/analysis, preferably on a quarterly basis, of the reciprocal accounts “Due from/Due to Other Funds” to ensure that necessary adjustments in the books of accounts are made on time.
We recommended the timely implementation of said project in order to ensure that the desired benefits/services could be delivered to the Municipality’s constituents on time.
observations.managementAction: Management said that physical accomplishment as of year-end is 100% completed

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality fast track the implementation of all ADM/AM projects funded from the national government agencies to ensure that the desired benefits/services could be delivered to the Municipality’s constituents on time.
observations.managementAction: Management said that physical accomplishment as of year-end is 100% completed

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly comply with the Supreme Court decision and National Budget Circular No. 476-01 dated September 20, 2001 on the reversion of the unexpended balance of the PDAF to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund. Likewise, comply with the provisions of National Budget Circular No. 476-01 and COA Circular No. 94-013.
observations.reason: A portion of the amount was already returned to the National Treasury while. The balance is still on- process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to provide the audit team of a duly accomplished Implementation Schedule for evaluation as to the timeliness of the implementation of the project as per Memorandum of Agreement (MOA). Likewise, identified priority programs/projects and activities (PPAs) of the Municipality must be implemented immediately for the timely benefits that can be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction: Management said that physical accomplishment was already 100% completed and that they already sent a letter request to DOH for the remaining fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management monitor the timely implementation of projects in accordance to the set date of completion and to record the disbursement in accordance to existing rules and regulations.
observations.reason: Management is still on the process of compiling documents relative to the fund transfers.

observations.managementAction: Management said that that the affected accounts were already adjusted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal MDRRMO include in the LDRRMF Investment Plan, in a separate caption, the list of projects and activities to be funded from the unexpended LDRRMF in previous years of the Trust Fund. We also recommend the implementation of the projects in the LDRRMFIP which are intended to contribute in strengthening the capacity of the LGU to respond to disasters.
observations.reason: Management already instructed the MDRRMO to come up with listing of PPAs with unexpended budget and that implementation of PPAs will depend on actual necessity and prevailing conditions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE, together with the members of the Municipal Waste Management Board, fully implement the establishment of a sanitary landfill, to ensure the protection of the public health and environment of the Municipality’s constituents.
observations.reason: Management said that bidding for consultancy services for the design of Sanitary Landfill is underway

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

submit a valid justification including proofs and legal bases relative to the engagement of the services of a private lawyer. Otherwise, rescind the contract for being contrary to laws and regulations and refund in favor of the government  expenses incurred thereon; 

Submit the Accomplishment Report which was used as the basis for the remuneration; and 
stop the hiring of private lawyers and resort to the services of the Provincial Legal Officer to represent them in cases where the Municipality is not a party adverse to the Provincial Government.
observations.managementAction: Demand Letters were sent to barangays which they complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that grant of professional fees to the Doctor to the Barrio in the succeeding months shall be withheld with the corresponding tax taking into consideration compliance of the documentary requirement in determining the applicable rate as stipulated in the provisions of Section 2.57.2 of RR No. 11-2018.
observations.managementAction: Management already deducted the necessary withholding taxes.

observations.reason: The Accounting already adjusted the accounts and now preparing the necessary report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Accounting Office to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to arrive at the correct balance of the account Cash in Treasury. Likewise, the Accountable Officers must reconcile regularly as to the actual cash on hand and her deposits to avoid errors that resulted to negative balance.
observations.managementAction: Management already made the necessary adjustments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that during the budgeting process, the respective focal persons in each program/projects/activities of the Municipality must properly plan their programs and use appropriate accounts. Likewise, the accounting office must adhere strictly in recording expenses using prescribed account codes and titles for the books of accounts to be reflective of correct information relative to transactions of the agency. In all instances, the “Other MOE” account shall be appropriately used solely to record transactions for which they are prescribed under COA rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Management already used the proper accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the timely implementation of said project in order to ensure that the desired benefits/services could be delivered to the Municipality’s constituents on time.

We recommended that the Municipality fast track the implementation of all ADM/AM projects funded from the national government agencies to ensure that the desired benefits/services could be delivered to the Municipality’s constituents on time.

We recommended that management strictly comply with the Supreme Court decision and National Budget Circular No. 476-01 dated September 20, 2001 on the reversion of the unexpended balance of the PDAF to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund. Likewise, comply with the provisions of National Budget Circular No. 476-01 and COA Circular No. 94-013.

We recommended management to provide the audit team of a duly accomplished Implementation Schedule for evaluation as to the timeliness of the implementation of the project as per Memorandum of Agreement (MOA). Likewise, identified priority programs/projects and activities (PPAs) of the Municipality must be implemented immediately for the timely benefits that can be derived therefrom.

We recommended that management monitor the timely implementation of projects in accordance to the set date of completion and to record the disbursement in accordance to existing rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal MDRRMO include in the LDRRMF Investment Plan, in a separate caption, the list of projects and activities to be funded from the unexpended LDRRMF in previous years of the Trust Fund. We also recommend the implementation of the projects in the LDRRMFIP which are intended to contribute in strengthening the capacity of the LGU to respond to disasters.

We recommended that the LCE, together with the members of the Municipal Waste Management Board, fully implement the establishment of a sanitary landfill, to ensure the protection of the public health and environment of the Municipality’s constituents.

We recommended that Management:
submit a valid justification including proofs and legal bases relative to the engagement of the services of a private lawyer. Otherwise, rescind the contract for being contrary to laws and regulations and refund in favor of the government  expenses incurred thereon;
Submit the Accomplishment Report which was used as the basis for the remuneration; and
stop the hiring of private lawyers and resort to the services of the Provincial Legal Officer to represent them in cases where the Municipality is not a party adverse to the Provincial Government.

We recommended that grant of professional fees to the Doctor to the Barrio in the succeeding months shall be withheld with the corresponding tax taking into consideration compliance of the documentary requirement in determining the applicable rate as stipulated in the provisions of Section 2.57.2 of RR No. 11-2018.

We recommend the Accounting Office to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to arrive at the correct balance of the account Cash in Treasury. Likewise, the Accountable Officers must reconcile regularly as to the actual cash on hand and her deposits to avoid errors that resulted to negative balance.

We recommended that during the budgeting process, the respective focal persons in each program/projects/activities of the Municipality must properly plan their programs and use appropriate accounts. Likewise, the accounting office must adhere strictly in recording expenses using prescribed account codes and titles for the books of accounts to be reflective of correct information relative to transactions of the agency. In all instances, the “Other MOE” account shall be appropriately used solely to record transactions for which they are prescribed under COA rules and regulations.
We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular 92-389 to maintain a sound internal control in the disbursement of public funds.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has provided instructions to concerned employee to stamp “PAID” all vouchers and supporting documents

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to fully utilize BUB funds from National Government Agencies for the benefit of the constituents and to ensure the proper use thereof in accordance with the guidelines under DBM-DILG-DSWD-NAPC JMC No. 7.
observations.reason: The bidding for the said project failed twice due to no takers. And after the successful awarding of the project to a contractor, delay in the delivery of materials caused the delay in the implementation of the project.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and concerned officials adhere strictly to the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 to avoid issuance of disallowance. All programs/projects and accomplishments should partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures.
observations.reason: Although management asserted that the observation was fully implemented, audit showed that some identified projects still violated said rules and regulations cited in the observation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit the municipality’s LDRRMP to the Office of the Civil Defense for review in compliance to the above-mentioned rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: In compliance to the provisions of  RA No. 101211, management already forwarded a copy of the LDRRMP to the OCD to which a certification was returned to them acknowledging the LDRRMP valid and consistent with provisions of the said regulation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE, together with the members of the Municipal Waste Management Board, fully implement the establishment of a sanitary landfill, to ensure the protection of the public health and environment of the Municipality’s constituents. Moreover, management shall include as one of the priority projects in their Annual Investment Plan said program and consider soliciting funds from Other National Government Agencies.
observations.reason: The LGU included in its 2018 Annual Budget appropriations for the preparation of a Technical Plan of a Proposed Sanitary Landfill. The LGU has also consulted with the DENR, EMB and MGB to request for technical assistance for the assessment of possible site for the proposed landfill. The LGU has also requested to include the construction of a sanitary landfill on the 20% EDF of the Provincial Government for the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to provide us a duly accomplished Implementation Schedule for evaluation as to the timeliness of the implementation of the project as per Memorandum of Agreement (MOA).
observations.reason: The implementation of the said project cannot proceed until all materials and aggregates are delivered. The late delivery of materials resulted to the delay of implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to strictly adhere with the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-01 in the planning and implementation of its 20% Development Fund. Proper planning must be made to avoid possible issuance of disallowance.
observations.reason: Although management already improved, there still few transactions violating the said regulation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board help assist the barangays to establish materials recovery facility that will receive the mixed solid waste for final sorting, segregation, composting or recycling before transferring the residual wastes to sanitary landfill. Likewise, use the transferred equipments for intended purpose of the program.
observations.reason: The MLGU appropriated  funds for the purchase of equipment to be issued to the barangays for the collection of recyclable wastes as part of the operation of the barangay MRFs. Other measures were also implemented by the LGU to enhance their solid Waste Management  Programs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Accounting Office to prepare necessary adjustments on the account Due from LGU as a result of the submission of a duly verified liquidation report. Likewise, management must oblige the other barangays to prepare and submit liquidation reports to the Auditor’s Office for verification.
observations.managementAction: Demand Letters were sent to barangays which they complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the accounting and treasury offices withhold taxes from the compensation, benefits and incentives granted to employees that exceeded the ceiling of P82,000.00. Remittance to the Bureau of Internal Revenue of the applicable taxes on the additional year-end benefits, Performance Enhancement Incentive, Collective Negotiation Agreement incentives and Economic Relief Assistance is also enjoined to avoid further penalties and surcharges. Likewise, submit Confirmation of Exemption as required in the BIR Revenue Memorandum Order No. 23-2014 dated June 20, 2014.
observations.managementAction: Management required all employees to pay the necessary taxes to which they complied. And succeeding benefits that were subjected to tax were already deducted with the corresponding taxes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to submit legal basis for granting Economic Relief Assistance. Likewise, stop the practice of paying allowances which do not have legal basis to avoid possible issuance of disallowance.
observations.managementAction: Management stopped the grant of benefits to all employees without legal basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management exert extra effort to cause the collection of the said receivables and to avail of administrative and legal remedies to effect collection.
observations.reason: A partial amount of the loans granted to individuals have already been collected. Another set of demand letters were already served to persons liable and the LGU is awaiting for their settlement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to direct the designated property officer to facilitate the conduct of physical count in order to come up with correct Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment.  The report must be reconciled with the balances of the  account at the Municipal Accounting Office, copy furnished the Office of the Auditor. Likewise, the accounting office must record all kinds of receipt of assets, liabilities and capital outlay in order to reflect correct balances of asset account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management already conducted actual physical count of properties and a report was already prepared and submitted to COA Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipal government submit its GPB for the ensuing year to the DILG office for review and approval pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 013-01. The GAD Focal Person is advised to monitor and evaluate the implementation of programs and activities of the approved GPB.
observations.managementAction: GAD Plan and Budget of the municipality for 2018 was submitted to DILG for review and approval for which an approved one was returned to the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that proper charging must be strictly observed. Likewise, the Accounting Office must report/record the correct account in the books in order to reflect the correct amounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Disbursements of supplies were lodged and reported to their appropriate account codes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management withhold taxes on all government procurement except those specifically specified under the Revenue Memorandum Order mentioned above to avoid penalties.
observations.managementAction: Management already withheld the necessary taxes in the procurement of goods.

observations.recommendation: 1.3 We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular 92-389 to maintain a sound internal control in the disbursement of public funds.

2.4 We recommended management to fully utilize BUB funds from National Government Agencies for the benefit of the constituents and to ensure the proper use thereof in accordance with the guidelines under DBM-DILG-DSWD-NAPC JMC No. 7.

3.10 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and concerned officials adhere strictly to the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 to avoid issuance of disallowance. All programs/projects and accomplishments should partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures.
4.4 We recommended that management submit the municipality’s LDRRMP to the Office of the Civil Defense for review in compliance to the above-mentioned rules and regulations.

We recommended that the LCE, together with the members of the Municipal Waste Management Board, fully implement the establishment of a sanitary landfill, to ensure the protection of the public health and environment of the Municipality’s constituents. 8.10 We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board help assist the barangays to establish materials recovery facility that will receive the mixed solid waste for final sorting, segregation, composting or recycling before transferring the residual wastes to sanitary landfill. Likewise, use the transferred equipments for intended purpose of the program.

9.7 We recommended the Accounting Office to prepare necessary adjustments on the account Due from LGU as a result of the submission of a duly verified liquidation report. Likewise, management must oblige the other barangays to prepare and submit liquidation reports to the Auditor’s Office for verification.

10.7 We recommended that the accounting and treasury offices withhold taxes from the compensation, benefits and incentives granted to employees that exceeded the ceiling of P82,000.00. Remittance to the Bureau of Internal Revenue of the applicable taxes on the additional year-end benefits, Performance Enhancement Incentive, Collective Negotiation Agreement incentives and Economic Relief Assistance is also enjoined to avoid further penalties and surcharges. 14.7 We recommended that the municipal government submit its GPB for the ensuing year to the DILG office for review and approval pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 013-01. The GAD Focal Person is advised to monitor and evaluate the implementation of programs and activities of the approved GPB.

We recommended management to ensure that all process and documentations prescribed by the above cited laws, rules and regulation are duly complied with to establish the validity, necessity and effectiveness in consonance with the purpose sought to be attained thru said undertakings.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented
observations.reason: Management already prepared and submitted LDRRMP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to provide us a duly accomplished Implementation Schedule for evaluation as to the timeliness of the implementation of the project as per Memorandum of Agreement (MOA).
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented
observations.reason: Some MOA between the LGU and source agency of downloaded funds were still not properly filled up.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to stop the practice of paying travelling expenses to employees hired under the job order (JO) contract of service to avoid possible issuance of suspension/disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: Fully implemented
observations.reason: No Job Order was given travel order for official business.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board officials strictly comply with the above mentioned rules and regulations in identifying programs/projects/activities chargeable against SEF to serve the educational needs of the province’s public schools.
observations.managementAction: Fully implemented
observations.reason: Management was compliant with DECS-DBM DILG JMC, thus, expenses were in line with the provisions of the regulation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive exert extra effort to fully implement the establishment of a sanitary landfill, to ensure the protection of the public health and environment of the Municipality’s constituents.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: No sanitary landfill had been established because the LGU is still waiting for response from DENR for their application for extension of the site.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management require the persons liable/responsible for disallowances to settle their accounts. If warranted, management could avail of Administrative and Civil remedies to effect payment.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented
observations.reason: The LGU have partially settled the balance of audit disallowance. As of December 31, 2017, only the amount of P4,979.75 remained unsettled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC, Municipal Accountant classify and record disbursements in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts to reflect the correct balance of accounts in the financial statement.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Management did not reclassify the transaction to the correct account. But we noted that repayment was regularly made through salary deduction.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board officials strictly follow the prescribed rules and regulations as mandated in order to serve the educational needs of the municipality’s public schools.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: No physical inventory taking of PPE was conducted during the year, thus, no report was prepared and submitted to the COA Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund for the purpose of achieving desirable socio-economic development and environmental management results. All expenditures partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures pursuant to the DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Various expenditures charged against the 20% DF made during the year were still not in accordance with DILG DBM JMC prescribed during the year and onwards.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the OIC-Accountant to record the donated vehicle in the agency books to reflect the true and actual balance of PPE account in the financial statement.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Management had not caused the recording of the donated vehicle.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the ICO-Municipal Accountant submit the Municipal accounts and financial reports within the prescribed period as required by the above-mentioned rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented
observations.reason: The Accounting Office made sure that accounts were submitted within due date although there were few months where accounts were delayed for few days.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management provide a legal basis to establish the propriety of the claim and to strictly comply with the provisions of the above mentioned Circular, otherwise, refund the amount paid for the overtime services.
observations.reason: The Management insisted that job order contract personnel were not among the coverage of Budget Circular No. 10 which was the criteria cited by the audit team in the Audit Observation Memorandum. Furthermore, they reasoned that claim of overtime services were only for the months of November to January during the preparation of year-end reports and not all throughout the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board officials strictly follow the prescribed rules and regulations as mandated in order to serve the educational needs of the municipality’s public schools.
observations.reason: The management still charges expenses against the Special Education Fund that are not under the mandate of the Local Government Code.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management return the unutilized funds transfer to the Bureau of Treasury pursuant to COA Circular 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.
observations.reason: Management has yet to revert the unexpended balance of the Disbursement Acceleration Program (DAP) to the Bureau of Treasury.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management comply with the provision of RA No. 9003 or the ecological solid waste management of 2000.
observations.reason: Management said that they are segregating all the solid wastes of the Municipality but explained to us that due to lack of sufficient funds to support the operation of a sanitary landfill it would be difficult for the municipality to comply with the above-mentioned rules and regulation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical inventory of all the Municipality’s properties and to reconcile the results with the accounting records to ascertain the correctness and existence of the reported balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management informed the Audit Team that they are now in the process of accounting all the properties to reconcile the property cards of the supply officer with the ledger cards of the accounting unit and assured us that they shall comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended each officials and employees of the municipality to refund the amount received as Productivity Enhancement Incentive (PEI) in excess of P5,000.00 or for a total amount of P614,904.26.
observations.reason: Management does not have any comment regarding the audit observation.

observations.recommendation: 1.6	We recommended management to ensure that all process and documentations prescribed by the above cited laws, rules and regulation are duly complied with to establish the validity, necessity and effectiveness in consonance with the purpose sought to be attained thru said undertakings.
2.3	We recommended management to provide us a duly accomplished Implementation Schedule for evaluation as to the timeliness of the implementation of the project as per Memorandum of Agreement (MOA).

3.4	We recommended management to stop the practice of paying travelling expenses to employees hired under the job order (JO) contract of service to avoid possible issuance of suspension/disallowance in audit.

4.9	We recommended that the Local School Board officials strictly comply with the above mentioned rules and regulations in identifying programs/projects/activities chargeable against SEF to serve the educational needs of the province’s public schools.

5.6	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive exert extra effort to fully implement the establishment of a sanitary landfill, to ensure the protection of the public health and environment of the Municipality’s constituents.

6.5	We recommended that the management require the persons liable/responsible for disallowances to settle their accounts. If warranted, management could avail of Administrative and Civil remedies to effect payment.

7.3	We recommended that the OIC, Municipal Accountant classify and record disbursements in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts to reflect the correct balance of  accounts in the financial statement.

8.4	We recommended Management to submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the auditor within the prescribed period and reconcile the same with the accounting records to ascertain the correctness and existence of the reported balances in the financial statements.

9.7	We recommended that management ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund for the purpose of achieving desirable socio-economic development and environmental management results. All expenditures partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures pursuant to the DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.

10.5	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the OIC-Accountant to record the donated vehicle in the agency books to reflect the true and actual balance of PPE account in the financial statement.
11.7	We recommended that the ICO-Municipal Accountant submit the Municipal accounts and financial reports within the prescribed period as required by the above-mentioned rules and regulations.

We recommended that management stop the practice of paying honorarium out of Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses account for the payment of honoraria and to adhere strictly with the provisions of the above cited regulations to avoid possible issuance of notice of disallowance.
observations.managementAction: The Management informed the Audit Team that the reason why honoraria were charged to Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses was because they were not aware that the savings as specified under the Budget Circular No. 2007-003 pertains only to savings under the Personnel Services. The management however agreed with the audit observation and adheres with the provisions of DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-003 dated November 29, 2007 and DBM Budget Circular No. 2004-5A dated March 23, 2004.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management stop the practice of transferring cash advances from one accountable officer to another and to adhere strictly to the above cited regulations on the granting and utilization of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Management has already stopped the practice of transferring cash advance from one accountable officer to another.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer (LDRRMO) submit monthly the report on the sources and utilization of the LDRRMF in compliance with Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed with the audit observation and is now submitting the required reports on the sources and utilization of LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that all job order employees assigned at different offices of the municipality be required to prepare and submit the necessary accomplishment reports and job order contract as supporting document to the payment of salaries and wages. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant properly review the completeness of supporting documents before processing the financial transactions of the agency to avoid possible suspension and/or disallowance thereof in audit.
observations.managementAction: The necessary accomplishment reports and job order contracts were already attached as supporting documents except for those laborers/job order employees charged against the 20% development fund wherein quarterly accomplishment report is submitted by the municipal engineering office that would serve as the laborers accomplishments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant classify and record expenses in accordance with the Manual on the New Government Accounting System in order to reflect the correct balance of the account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Management said that these wages charged to capital outlay were direct cost and the expenses were directly attributed to the property and therefore capitalized, however labor payrolls does not specifically state the name of the projects, works to be done and the quantity/volume of the project for which the labor has been utilized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to follow the standard procedures in the disbursement of funds for the acquisition of equipment. Likewise, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to pay transactions of this nature through checks for control purposes.
observations.managementAction: Management now follows the standard procedures in the disbursement of funds for the acquisition of equipment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the ICO-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer submit the Municipal accounts and financial reports within the prescribed period as required by the above-mentioned rules and regulations for verification.
observations.managementAction: Submission of reports by management have improved as compared with previous years, however, we noted that there were still accounts that were slightly delayed especially accounts and reports for the  3rd and 4th quarter of the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management require the persons liable/responsible for disallowances to settle their accounts through the availment of Administrative and Civil remedies as provided by law.
observations.managementAction: Management continuously collects payment from persons liable and will exert more effort to recover and settle the audit disallowances of the officials and employees of the Municipality.
